narrative:
Alex Freeman is a Right-Back for the United States. His overall rating of 7.29/10 places him in the top 2% of right-backs at this tournament, reflecting strong form. His rating range of 6.91–7.67 indicates consistent performance across matches, with 73% of his rating derived from club form over the last two seasons.

Freeman demonstrates remarkable attacking efficiency at this World Cup, with 0.241 goals per 90 minutes and 0.241 assists per 90 minutes. This output significantly exceeds his expected goals of 0.109 and expected assists of 0.028 per 90 minutes in the tournament. Defensively, he contributes with 3.378 tackles and 3.619 clearances per 90 minutes.

Freeman is proving to be a critical two-way Right-Back for the United States, logging 373 minutes in the tournament. Scouts should monitor if his exceptional World Cup attacking contributions, which heavily outweigh his club-level xG and xA, are sustainable. Fans should watch for his continued influence on both ends of the pitch in upcoming matches.
